title: Token Revocation
description: Implement POST /api/v1/auth/token/revoke per RFC 7009. Add revoked token JTI to Redis blocklist (TTL = original token expiry). Introspection checks blocklist before returning active=true. Support cascading revocation of refresh tokens.
validation: Adds revoked token JTI to Redis blocklist ✅
Blocklist TTL matches original token expiry ✅
Supports cascading revocation of refresh tokens ✅
Tests verify revoked tokens return active=false ✅

implementation: Token revocation endpoint fully implemented at POST /api/v1/auth/token/revoke
Follows RFC 7009 - idempotent revocation, returns 200 OK regardless of token validity
Revoked tokens added to Redis blocklist with key format 'revoked:{jti}'
TTL for blocklist entries matches token's natural expiration time
Introspection checks blocklist before validation, returns active=false for revoked tokens
Cascading revocation implemented: revoking access token also revokes refresh token and vice versa
Token pair relationships stored in Redis during minting with keys 'token_pair:access:{jti}' and 'token_pair:refresh:{jti}'
Cache invalidation for paired tokens handled via 'jti_to_cache:{jti}' mappings
Audit events created for all revocation operations
testing: test_revoke_token_endpoint - verifies endpoint returns 200 OK and creates audit event
test_token_revocation_with_real_redis - end-to-end test with real Redis
test_cascading_revocation_access_token_revokes_refresh - verifies cascading from access to refresh
test_cascading_revocation_refresh_token_revokes_access - verifies cascading from refresh to access
features: Cascading revocation ensures both access and refresh tokens are invalidated together
Cache invalidation prevents stale introspection results
Token pair mappings cleaned up after revocation
Supports partial token information during cascading (uses max TTL when exact expiry unknown)
Logging includes detailed information about cascading operations
